# Surp v1.0.1

Surp `v1.0.1` is a patch release for the Rust-backed Surp serialization
toolkit. It promotes the Python model and introspection APIs, refreshes the
README into a complete project guide, and ships committed benchmark charts for
the release.

## Highlights

- Adds `surp.model`, an RFC-001-native Python class schema and validation layer
  inside the existing `surp` package.
- Adds native-backed Python view/model APIs: `SurpValue`, `RfcDocument`,
  `RfcDecodedCbf`, `RfcValue`, and related RFC model classes.
- Adds JSON-like Rust introspection helpers for v1 `Value` and
  zero-copy `SurpValue<'_>`.
- Adds RFC-001 AST introspection helpers for documents, products, sums,
  sequences, tensors, streams, and decoded CBF metadata.
- Adds Protocol Buffers coverage to the Rust benchmark harness.
- Adds generated SVG benchmark charts and release benchmark artifacts under
  `docs/assets/bench/v1.0.1`.
- Rewrites the root README with installation, Rust/Python/CLI usage, RFC-001,
  Python model usage, benchmark, and local development sections.
- Updates Rust workspace package metadata to `1.0.1`.

## Benchmark Artifacts

The release benchmark was run with:

```sh
cargo run -p surp-bench --release -- --mode full --output docs/assets/bench/v1.0.1 --version v1.0.1
```

Generated artifacts include:

- `docs/assets/bench/v1.0.1/raw.json`
- `docs/assets/bench/v1.0.1/summary.csv`
- `docs/assets/bench/v1.0.1/size_comparison.md`
- `docs/assets/bench/v1.0.1/charts/serialized-size.svg`
- `docs/assets/bench/v1.0.1/charts/encode-throughput.svg`
- `docs/assets/bench/v1.0.1/charts/decode-throughput.svg`

## Fixes

- Fixes RFC-001 empty-map formatting so canonical CTN stays parseable and
  normalization remains idempotent.
- Fixes Pyright analysis by providing a typed private stub for the native
  extension import.
- Fixes RFC-001 query overload stubs so `as_ctn=True` and `as_ctn=False` are
  distinguishable to type checkers.

## Notes

- Release tag: `v1.0.1`
- Release title: `Surp v1.0.1`
- v1 wire compatibility is unchanged.
- RFC-001 remains additive and separate from v1 `.surp` files.
